summary: First of three parts: In a region of Alaska where culture and commerce both come from fishing nets, something essential is missing.

title: I’m married to an extreme extrovert, and I’m starting to worry that by myself I’ll never be enough
summary: Relationship advice: We hardly ever have experiences just the two of us. Whenever we plan anything — and I do mean anything — he is texting and calling buddies to join us. And people always, always join us.

title: Diversity of scientific disciplines kept the Geophysical Institute at UAF growing for decades
summary: Launched after World War II with a focus on auroras and space physics, scientists at the institute went on to study earthquakes, volcanoes and the atmosphere.

title: Waterfowl season brings warm memories from a lifetime pursuit of ducks and geese (and another reason to talk about Rigby)
summary: There’s an appeal to sitting in duck blinds in cold, driving rain and crawling hundreds of yards across stubble fields in the dark.

title: If you plan to travel abroad from Alaska, stay on top of shifting pandemic restrictions
summary: Here in the U.S., there are no specific restrictions on travel. But if you’re on the move, you’re wise to watch how COVID-19 is affecting the communities you’re visiting.

title: Texas created a blueprint for abortion restrictions. Republican-controlled states may follow suit.
summary: After Texas enacted a first-of-its-kind near-total abortion ban, advocates on both sides anticipate a domino effect in Republican-controlled statehouses, as the nation has reached a critical moment for abortion rights.

title: Alaska Gov. Mike Dunleavy replaces state’s transportation commissioner
summary: A 20-year state employee will replace John MacKinnon, one of the governor’s original cabinet selections.

title: Doctors and pharmacists ramp up calls for Americans to stop using ivermectin to treat COVID-19
summary: Health experts and medical groups are warning that the use of the drug, which is used to kill worms and other parasites in humans and animals, can cause harmful side effects and that there’s little evidence it helps.

title: Alaska Supreme Court confirms an end to 2 limits on cash in political campaigns
summary: Alaska can’t exempt itself from the U.S. Supreme Court’s ruling on Citizens United, state judges said.

title: Here’s what we know about the mu variant
summary: Cases of the WHO-designated “variant of interest” have been reported in the United States, Colombia, South Korea and parts of Europe.

title: EPA details all the ways climate change will hit U.S. racial minorities the hardest
summary: If the planet warms 2 degrees Celsius, a new report warns, Black people are 40 percent more likely to currently live in places where extreme temperatures will cause more deaths.

title: U.S. COVID death toll hits 1,500 a day amid delta scourge
summary: ‘A perfect storm of viral changes and behavioral changes,’ is driving pandemic’s fourth wave in the U.S.

title: U.S. COVID death toll hits 1,500 a day amid delta scourge
summary: ‘A perfect storm of viral changes and behavioral changes,’ is driving pandemic’s fourth wave in the U.S.

title: So-called ‘QAnon Shaman’ pleads guilty to charges in storming of US Capitol
summary: Jacob Chansley, an Arizona man who sported face paint, no shirt and a furry hat with horns when he joined the mob that stormed the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, pleaded guilty Friday to a felony charge.

title: Curious Alaska: Amid all the quirky things for sale at the state fair, what’s with hot tubs?
summary: A hot tub is a high-dollar item to buy between curly fries and the rollercoaster, but salespeople swear a surprising number of fairgoers take the plunge.

title: US hiring slows as coronavirus delta variant weakens travel and tourism industries
summary: America’s employers added just 235,000 jobs in August, a surprisingly weak gain after two months of robust hiring.

title: ISIS militant admits involvement in torture, killings of American hostages
summary: He is the first held accountable in U.S. courts for killing journalists and aid workers in Syria.

title: All new hires at Alaska Airlines must be vaccinated against COVID-19, company says
summary: Employees who show proof of vaccination will receive $200, Alaska Airlines said in a statement.

title: Alaska launches series of $49,000 awards to encourage COVID-19 vaccinations
summary: Prizes will be awarded weekly for newly vaccinated entrants through Oct. 30. Those vaccinated before Sept. 2 will be eligible for a one-time drawing.

title: Gov. Dunleavy adds telehealth, nurse licensing bills to special session agenda to mixed reactions from lawmakers
summary: Some Alaska legislators are skeptical that the bills would help the state address the current COVID-19 surge, as Dunleavy says.

title: Seafood processor Peter Pan will require employees to be vaccinated
summary: The mandatory vaccinations do not apply to crew members in the fishing fleet, which operate separately from Peter Pan.

title: Anglers still have a chance to turn their catches into cash at derbies in Valdez and Kenai
summary: The Valdez halibut and silver salmon derbies end Sunday night, and the Kenai silver salmon derby is set for Sept. 14-19.

title: Pollution cleanup from sunken ship near Kodiak is costing millions
summary: Officials said that as of last week, there had been no reports of oiled wildlife or impacts to the shoreline.

title: Fun things coming up: Dance in the botanical garden, the Girdwood Fungus Fair and closing weekend for the Alaska State Fair
summary: Also, First Friday gallery openings and theatrical performances on climate change outside the Eagle River Nature Center.

title: Almond grape cake is great for packing, snacking and topping with lemon cream
summary: Recipe: This almond-laced snacking cake packs up well for school lunches, hiking and camping.

title: Divided Supreme Court leaves restrictive Texas abortion law in place
summary: A deeply divided Supreme Court is allowing a Texas law that bansmost abortions to remain in force, stripping most women of the right toan abortion in the nation’s second-largest state.

title: Anchorage School District had no plans to report COVID-19 cases school by school. That changed this week.
summary: Community pressure prompted the decision to share more coronavirus details at ASD, until now the state’s only large district that didn’t plan to release school-specific case updates.

title: Anchorage School District had no plans to report COVID-19 cases school by school. That changed this week.
summary: Community pressure prompted the decision to share more coronavirus details at ASD, until now the state’s only large district that didn’t plan to release school-specific case updates.

title: Anchorage schools superintendent recommends renaming Fairview Elementary after district’s first Black teacher and principal
summary: Dr. Etheldra Davis worked for over 30 years as an educator in the state, including at Fairview Elementary School. Davis died in November at age 89.

title: Former Alaska attorney general John Havelock dies at age 89
summary: Active in Democratic politics, he helped institute limited-entry fishing and guided the first oil tax program on the North Slope.

title: Effort underway to rescue girls soccer team from Afghanistan
summary: The teen girls and their families could be targeted by the Taliban — not just because women and girls are forbidden to play sports, but because they were advocates for girls and active members of their communities.

title: Anchorage Mayor Bronson revokes policies for city workers intended to support new parents
summary: One policy was enacted by former Acting Mayor Austin Quinn-Davidson one day before Dave Bronson took office.

title: Group seeking to split Eagle River from Anchorage isn’t asking for city money after all
summary: The EaglExit secession movement is still asking to be considered for federally funded grants distributed by the city.

title: Two teachers die from COVID in same week, leading Texas school to close
summary: The district didn’t say whether the teachers were vaccinated. An executive order by Gov. Greg Abbott prohibits mandates on face coverings in schools.

title: Texas ban on most abortions after six weeks takes effect with no word from US Supreme Court
summary: The law would be the most dramatic restriction on abortion rights since the high court’s landmark Roe v. Wade decision legalized abortion across the country in 1973.

title: Strong winds push California wildfire closer to edge of South Lake Tahoe
summary: Flames raced across treetops and through drought-stricken vegetation as firefighters scrambled early Wednesday to keep the Caldor Fire away from the resort city.

title: Hundreds of community members help paint Anchorage’s newest mural
summary: The 40-foot-wide mural created by artist Steve Gordon represents hope and community.

title: Alaska surpasses previous hospitalizations record and reports deaths of 6 with COVID-19, including woman in her 20s
summary: The state also reported more than 600 coronavirus cases as the demand for testing overwhelmed drive-thru sites around the state.

title: Alaska House votes down measures opposing vaccine mandates
summary: One proposal passed but was reversed after hospital officials sent urgent messages in opposition.

title: Biden calls U.S. airlift from Afghanistan a success and defends decision to end ‘forever war’
summary: President Biden is under heavy criticism, particularly from Republicans, for his handling of the evacuation, though it successfully airlifted more than 120,000 people from Kabul airport.

title: Emaciated seabirds are turning up dead on Western Alaska beaches for fifth straight summer
summary: Researchers and federal scientists still have no definitive explanation for the cause.

title: Anchorage Assembly overrides Mayor Bronson’s veto of new advisory board of people who have experienced homelessness
summary: The override passed in a 9-1 vote during a special meeting on Tuesday, marking the first time the Assembly has quashed a veto from Anchorage Mayor Dave Bronson.
